title: Equal Tree Partition

starterCode.python:

class Solution:

	def solve(self, A):

hints: Use post-order traversal to calculate subtree sums.
Store all subtree sums in a set while traversing.
Calculate the total sum of the entire tree.
If the total sum is odd, it's impossible to partition equally - return 0.
If the total sum is even, check if half of the total sum exists in the subtree sums set.
If half the sum exists in subtree sums, we can remove an edge to create two equal partitions.
Note: We don't include the root's subtree sum in the check (since removing root would leave nothing).
The key insight: if a subtree has sum = total_sum/2, removing the edge above it creates two equal partitions.
